environment_option_used: real local PostgreSQL 18.2 (native Windows binary), matching the precedent from AKARFINDER-OPENSERP-AUTOMATED-INGESTION-30MIN-1 Gate B
engine: PostgreSQL 18.2 (native Windows binary, local, disposable data directory, ephemeral, dropped after the gate)
postgres_version_query_result: PostgreSQL 18.2 on x86_64-windows, compiled by msvc-19.44.35222, 64-bit

instance.auth: trust (local-only disposable instance, no password used, no network exposure beyond localhost)

role_model.grants: USAGE on schema public + SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on openserp_query_rotation_state, openserp_engine_budget_state, and discovery_candidates (for the run-lock check) granted to all three roles, matching Supabase's actual default grant pattern -- RLS is the real access boundary.

migrations_applied: 20260716140000_create_discovery_candidates.sql (pre-existing, applied only to exercise run-lock's sentinel-row mechanism, unmodified by this mission)

test_3_anon_insert_rejected.actual: ERROR: new row violates row-level security policy for table "openserp_query_rotation_state"

test_4_authenticated_insert_rejected.actual: ERROR: new row violates row-level security policy for table "openserp_engine_budget_state"

test_5_service_role_seed.actual: 2 rotation rows, 3 budget rows inserted successfully

test_6_rotation_state_persists_across_in.expected: an update from a simulated 'run 1' is durably readable
test_6_rotation_state_persists_across_in.actual: gb-q1 updated: last_executed_at set, successful_run_count=1, discovery_yield=2, last_run_id=gb-run-1

test_7_run_lock_concurrency.expected: first acquire succeeds; a concurrent second acquire attempt on the same sentinel is rejected by the unique index (mutual exclusion); release (delete) leaves 0 lock rows
test_7_run_lock_concurrency.actual: first INSERT succeeded (1 row); second concurrent INSERT rejected: 'duplicate key value violates unique constraint discovery_candidates_idempotency_idx'; after delete, 0 lock rows remain

test_8_captcha_suspension_persists.expected: suspended_until and the dropped budget are readable from a fresh query, not dependent on in-process state
test_8_captcha_suspension_persists.actual: bing: suspended=true, current_budget=4; duckduckgo/ecosia: suspended=false, current_budget=12 (unaffected, matching applyRunOutcome's per-engine suspension design)

test_9_failed_transaction_rollback.expected: a transaction containing an invalid engine name (violating the CHECK constraint) rolls back entirely, including its own earlier valid statement -- no partial rows, no orphans
test_9_failed_transaction_rollback.actual: transaction aborted on the engine_check constraint violation; gb-q2's discovery_yield update (set to 999 inside the same transaction) was rolled back and reads 0; budget row count stayed at 3 (the invalid 'unknown_engine' row never persisted)

test_10_no_observations_no_multi_source_.expected: this mission creates and touches only 2 new tables -- source_offer_observations and property_clusters must not even exist in this gate's schema, proving structurally that neither an Observation nor a multi-source cluster could have been created here
test_10_no_observations_no_multi_source_.actual: to_regclass('source_offer_observations') IS NULL = true; to_regclass('property_clusters') IS NULL = true
